positions.definition: Under the Gun - First position after the big blind; earliest position to act preflop
Early Position - Early positions at the table, typically UTG through UTG+1/2
Middle Position - Positions between early position and hijack
Hijack - Position one seat before the cutoff
Cutoff - Position one seat before the button
Button - Dealer position; the best position at the table
Small Blind - Position to the left of the button
positions.example: SB: tricky (out of position the whole hand)

positions.definition: Big Blind - Position to the left of the small blind; posts the big blind
positions.example: Blinds are the only seats where you pay to play
LJ limps, you're in the CO

actions.definition: Raise First In - Being the first player to enter the pot with a raise after everyone folds to you
actions.example: RFI (Raise First In) means you are the first player to enter the pot with a raise

actions.definition: The re-raise versus a preflop open
actions.example: A 3-bet is the re-raise versus a preflop open
You have three options: fold, call, 4-bet

actions.definition: The first raise preflop (same as RFI)

actions.example: Suited broadways... Pocket pairs in position

actions.definition: To surrender the hand and forfeit any chips invested
To call the big blind preflop without raising
actions.example: ISO raising means raising after someone limps

actions.definition: To 3-bet after one or more players have already called the open
actions.example: If there are callers and you're squeezing, size up

actions.definition: Isolation Raise - Raising after someone limps with the goal of isolating the weakest player
actions.example: ISO raising means raising after someone limps with the goal of isolating the weakest player

actions.definition: To continue in a hand when facing a bet or raise

hand_notations.example: UTG KTo, open CO KTo, open BTN KTo

hand_notations.definition: High cards T, J, Q, K, A (also called broadway cards)
hand_notations.example: Short stacks favor Broadways and pairs

hand_notations.definition: Two cards of the same rank (AA, KK, QQ, etc.)
Both cards are the same suit
hand_notations.example: Suited helps, but it doesn't magically make a hand good

concepts.definition: Your share of the pot based on your chance to win at showdown
The ratio of the call amount to the total pot
concepts.example: Pot odds tell you the minimum equity you need (~27%)

concepts.definition: Minimum Defense Frequency - The minimum percentage of hands to continue with to remain unexploitable; MDF = pot / (pot + bet)
concepts.example: MDF is a guardrail, not a religion

concepts.definition: The expected value gained when your bet causes opponents to fold
The additional equity you expect to gain from future betting if you hit your hand
concepts.example: Deep stacks reward hands that can make hidden strong hands

concepts.definition: The expected loss when you make a hand that is likely second-best
concepts.example: Dominated hands lead to reverse IO

concepts.definition: How much of your raw equity you actually realize based on position and playability
concepts.example: Equity realization drops, especially out of position

concepts.definition: Stack-to-Pot Ratio - Effective stack size divided by pot size
When an opponent's hand has you outkicked
Cards you hold that reduce the likelihood of opponent holding certain hands
concepts.example: A5s as a bluff with blockers

concepts.definition: How well a hand realizes equity postflop

strategy_terms.definition: A 3-bet range that includes premiums + strong hands + hands that benefit from protection
strategy_terms.example: Use linear when you expect calls

strategy_terms.definition: A 3-bet range at the extremes: strong value hands + semi-bluffs
strategy_terms.example: Use polar when you expect folds, especially in position

strategy_terms.definition: A range that sits between linear and polarized
The entire set of hands a player would take a particular action with
The weakest hands in your range
How many big blinds are in play
The smallest stack between you and your opponent(s)
Short stack depth (typically under 40bb)
strategy_terms.example: Short stacks favor Broadways and pairs

strategy_terms.definition: Large stack depth (100bb+)
strategy_terms.example: Deep stacks reward hands that can make hidden strong hands

strategy_terms.definition: A pot contested by only two players
strategy_terms.example: Heads-up BB vs open: defend wide

strategy_terms.definition: A pot with three or more players remaining
strategy_terms.example: Multiway BB: tighten the offsuit trash

strategy_terms.definition: Isolation - Raising to force limpers out and play heads-up
strategy_terms.example: ISO raising means raising after someone limps

strategy_terms.definition: Calling preflop with a pocket pair hoping to flop a set
A 3-bet after other players have already called the open
strategy_terms.example: If there are callers and you're squeezing, size up

strategy_terms.definition: The amount you bet when raising first in
The amount you bet when re-raising
Your seat relative to the button
In Position - Acting after your opponent(s)
Out of Position - Acting before your opponent(s)
The fee taken by the poker room/online platform
strategy_terms.example: EP mistakes are expensive (rake + OOP + 3-bet pressure)

strategy_terms.definition: Playing fewer hands
strategy_terms.example: Early position is tight and disciplined

strategy_terms.definition: Playing more hands
Frequently betting and raising
Frequently calling rather than raising
A very tight player
A very loose and aggressive player
A player who calls too much and doesn't fold
Folding more than optimal
A player who bluffs less than optimal
A situation where you can win without risking additional chips
strategy_terms.example: Avoid giving BB a cheap freeroll
